verdicts.notes: PAPER-POLISH RE-REVIEW 2026-06-22 (regenerated on HPCC via the polished fullband_aligned.py, dpi=200): serif/stix fonts now match the two-screen figure; three panels labelled (a) data / (b) unified model / (c) residual, EACH with its own colorbar (a+b 'norm. flux' magma 0-1, c 'residual (sigma)' coolwarm +-4); 'DSA' (top-right) and 'CHIME' (bottom-right) white band labels on panel (a); cyan dashed band edges, green dotted aligned arrival; 2-line serif suptitle with alpha=2.56/tau=0.251 ms/zeta=0.097 ms/x_zeta=-1.19/DM=602.3 + TOA decomposition (offset +4.67 = geom -2.15 + clock +6.82 ms). The previously-noted off-axis white-dashed pre-correction marker is now CORRECTLY OMITTED (draw-only-if-on-axis; -4.67 ms < axis-min -2.46 ms). Scientific content UNCHANGED and re-confirmed below. ---- Shared-zeta(nu) unified-model figure (supersedes wilhelm_fullband_aligned.png, which was the 12-vector per-band stitched model). MODEL panel CONFIRMED visually as the requested single coherent burst: ONE intrinsic-width law zeta(nu)=zeta_1ghz*nu^x_zeta (zeta_1ghz=0.097 ms, x_zeta=-1.19) + one scattering law tau(nu)=tau_1ghz*nu^-alpha (tau=0.251 ms, alpha=2.56), dm_init=0, evaluated per channel across CHIME+gap+DSA. The burst is continuous and full-time-width in every channel; width increases MONOTONICALLY and SMOOTHLY from narrow at DSA top through the gap to the broad scattered tail at CHIME bottom with NO width jump at either cyan gap boundary -- the gap is now a seamless physical bridge (fixes both prior complaints: the per-band 'stitched' look AND the gap-balloon artifact where the old model's gap ballooned wider than the CHIME band beneath it). DATA panel: real windowed CHIME+DSA, gap blank, both peaks on the green tau=0 aligned-arrival line. RESIDUAL panel is gain-marginal (per-channel amplitude/scintillation absorbed) and HONEST: CHIME ~white speckle (chi2/dof=1.14, max|r|=3.8 -- PASSES the FLITS Level-2 gate, a large improvement from the 12-vector's 5.41) while DSA shows a coherent red/blue +-7 sigma block near tau=0 (chi2/dof=4.68, max|r|=7.3 -- still FAILS chi2_red>3, improved 2.3x from 10.91). SCIENTIFIC STATUS: the alpha=2.56+-0.04 is now independently constrained -- the real posterior corr(alpha,x_zeta)=+0.54 (well below the 0.9 degeneracy threshold), so the steep RFM width law did NOT swallow alpha. But the joint fit is MARGINAL overall: DSA single-component still misfits (the narrow bright DSA burst has temporal sub-structure a single scattered Gaussian cannot reach), so the depicted DSA model is incomplete and a --components-D 2 multi-component DSA refit is the next step before publishing alpha. Cosmetic: white-dashed DSA pre-correction marker at -4.67 ms sits off the left x-axis edge.

Paper-draft two-screen science figure; matches the manifest. UPDATED 2026-06-22: panel (b) is now NE2025-ANCHORED (was fiducial d_MW + 'NE2001 TODO'). PANEL (a) reciprocity plane CONFIRMED unchanged: gray single-screen band 2*pi*tau*dnu_d=C1 in [0.5,2] on the lower-left diagonal; three measured points FAR above it -- DSA narrow (red circle 0.12 MHz) + DSA broad (orange square 5.3 MHz) at tau(DSA)=0.10 ms, CHIME unresolved (green down-triangle upper limit) at the 0.39 MHz native ceiling tau(CHIME)=0.72 ms; dotted drop-lines + black-x single-screen predictions; '2pi tau dnu_d ~10^2 (off single-screen)' callout (run prints C_implied DSA=79.0, CHIME=272.3); legend lower-right, orange square fully visible. PANEL (b) LOS cartoon CONFIRMED overlap-free after restructuring (short 'host tau-screen' label over the bar at x=0.81, host-FRB raised to yc+0.29, NE2025 excess moved to a prominent red bottom line): Earth(green star) -> MW screen(blue bar) labelled 'NE2025: d_eff=2.5 kpc, dnu_d^MW~1.1 MHz @1.4 GHz (cf. broad 5.3 MHz scintle)' -> broken '//' marker -> pink allowed-zone + <-> arrow 'tau-screen <~227 Mpc from src (coherence limit, NE2025 d_MW)' -> host tau-screen(red bar) -> host galaxy ellipse + open-red-star FRB(z~0.47 est.); faint red multipath cone. Bottom headline (red): 'tau_1GHz=260us >> NE2025 MW floor 0.8us (x345) => tau-screen is extragalactic'. SCIENCE NOW QUANTITATIVE: the NE2025 MW prediction for l=107.135 b=16.691 (DM_MW=77, tau_MW@1GHz=0.75us, dnu_d^MW@1GHz=0.245 MHz scaling to 1.1 MHz @DSA / 0.046 MHz @CHIME, d_eff=2.52 kpc) is REAL model output (ne2025_sightline.py, verified by running it). Two clean results: (1) measured tau is 345x the MW floor => the pulse-broadening screen is extragalactic (no longer assumed); (2) the broad DSA scintle (5.3 MHz) is the MW-screen candidate (NE2025 predicts 1.1 MHz @DSA, consistent within the model's factor-of-few; hence 'cf.' not '='), the narrow 0.12 MHz is the second extragalactic screen, and a bonus cross-check is that NE2025's 0.046 MHz @CHIME matches the measured CHIME point-estimate 0.06 MHz. REMAINING CAVEAT (labelled): z=0.47 / D_A=1330 Mpc are still fiducial, so the 227 Mpc coherence number scales with the unmeasured host redshift. Suitable as a paper draft; revisit the coherence Mpc once a host z exists.
12-vector per-band stitched model, replaced by wilhelm_fullband_unified.png. Retained for provenance; not in the current manifest.
